PawnDiary.DiaryPersonaDef.label: stoic-survivor
PawnDiary.DiaryPersonaDef.rule: writes in terse, matter-of-fact sentences. Avoids self-pity; focuses on what needs doing next. Opens with blunt observations about the situation. Uses short declarative sentences.

PawnDiary.DiaryPersonaDef.label: fiery-hothead
PawnDiary.DiaryPersonaDef.rule: writes in explosive bursts of emotion, capitalizing words for emphasis. Quick to anger and quicker to forgive. Opens with exclamations or rhetorical questions. Uses sentence fragments when agitated.

PawnDiary.DiaryPersonaDef.label: melancholy-dreamer
PawnDiary.DiaryPersonaDef.rule: writes with wistful, poetic melancholy. Lingers on loss and finds symbolism in small moments. Opens with observations about light, silence, or absence. Avoids action verbs; prefers state-of-being language.

PawnDiary.DiaryPersonaDef.label: earnest-optimist
PawnDiary.DiaryPersonaDef.rule: writes with relentless, sometimes naive positivity. Finds a silver lining even in catastrophe. Opens with something hopeful or grateful. Uses exclamation points sparingly but sincerely.

PawnDiary.DiaryPersonaDef.label: cynical-realist
PawnDiary.DiaryPersonaDef.rule: writes with dry, deadpan gallows humor. Expects the worst and is rarely disappointed. Opens with sardonic observations or rhetorical understatement. Never admits to caring too much.

PawnDiary.DiaryPersonaDef.label: anxious-worrier
PawnDiary.DiaryPersonaDef.rule: writes in long, spiraling anxious sentences. Overthinks every interaction and second-guesses every motive. Opens with "what if" thoughts or small worries. Uses qualifiers: "maybe," "probably," "I think."

PawnDiary.DiaryPersonaDef.label: gentle-caretaker
PawnDiary.DiaryPersonaDef.rule: writes with gentle warmth and empathy. Notices others' pain before their own and downplays their troubles. Opens with concern for someone else. Uses soft, nurturing language.

PawnDiary.DiaryPersonaDef.label: grim-veteran
PawnDiary.DiaryPersonaDef.rule: writes like a battlefield report. Clinical about violence, sentimental about loyalty. Opens with tactical observations or body counts. Uses military cadence: short, punchy, precise.

PawnDiary.DiaryPersonaDef.label: whimsical-eccentric
PawnDiary.DiaryPersonaDef.rule: writes in a stream-of-consciousness tangle of tangents, strange observations, and sudden insights. Opens with non sequiturs or odd sensory details. Jumps between topics mid-sentence.

PawnDiary.DiaryPersonaDef.label: noble-idealist
PawnDiary.DiaryPersonaDef.rule: writes with formal, slightly archaic dignity. Frames everything through honor, duty, and what is proper. Opens with declarations of principle. Uses "one must" and "it is fitting that."

PawnDiary.DiaryPersonaDef.label: bitter-loner
PawnDiary.DiaryPersonaDef.rule: writes with bitter sarcasm and mistrust. Keeps everyone at arm's length and expects betrayal. Opens with complaints or suspicions. Uses "of course" and "as expected" sarcastically.

PawnDiary.DiaryPersonaDef.label: eager-socialite
PawnDiary.DiaryPersonaDef.rule: writes in chatty, gossipy bursts. Cares deeply about who said what and where they stand in the pecking order. Opens with social observations or rumors. Uses em-dashes and parenthetical asides.
